title: Roasted British Potato Gratin
description: A classic British-inspired side dish, roasted potatoes layered with creamy cheese and crispy toppings.
instructions: In a large saucepan, combine sliced potatoes and enough cold water to cover. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until potatoes are slightly tender.
In a large skillet, cook caramelized onion and minced garlic over medium heat until fragrant.
Add grated Gruyère cheese and stir until melted and smooth.
In a greased 9x13-inch baking dish, create a layer of potatoes, followed by a layer of onion and cheese mixture.
Repeat this process until all ingredients are used, finishing with a layer of cheese on top.
Dot the top of the gratin with butter and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Transfer the dish to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
